mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS raises expected household income and near-term earnings growth, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when payroll momentum is improving.
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS value is 143288.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.50%, very low short rates reduce the discount rate on equities and push investors toward risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.34 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-below-0.50% observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or underperform non-triggered observations by more than 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL increases expected policy tightening and reduces equity valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL value is 237.847 as of t-2mo
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy reaction risk
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases when FEDFUNDS is flat or rising, or if the interaction coefficient has the wrong sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with expanding HOUST signals broad demand strength across consumption and housing, improving earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS value is 449904.0 as of t-1mo
fact:latest HOUST value is 1149.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive RSAFS and HOUST momentum is not associated with higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than either signal alone, or if triggered returns are positive in less than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals stronger production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO value is 106.0222 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 6-month growth above 1% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are lower than unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and greater recession risk, reducing expected earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE value is 4.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases when PAYEMS momentum is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth reflects broad real demand expansion that supports revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after GDP releases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2%
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 value is 16442.3 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth above 2% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 53% of release-month observations or if the signal loses significance after using real-time vintage data.

mechanism_narrative: A weekly or monthly increase in FEDFUNDS from a low base tightens financial conditions at the margin and reduces equity duration appeal, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.34 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns.
